# Copilot Instructions

## When writing code (coding agent)

- Always create a feature branch — never push directly to main/master.
- Use conventional commit messages: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `chore:`, `content:`.
- Create a PR when work is complete, with a summary and test plan in the body.
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase. Read the project's README or CLAUDE.md if present.
- Do not add dependencies without justification.

## General style

- Prefer TypeScript strict mode conventions where applicable.
- Keep changes minimal and focused on the task.
- Do not refactor surrounding code unless asked.
